use-cases.title: Assessing OpenStreetMap Completeness for Management of Natural Disaster by Means of Remote Sensing: A Case Study of Three Small Island States (Haiti, Dominica and St. Lucia)
use-cases.description: Researchers use remote sensing to evaluate OpenStreetMap building-footprint completeness for natural disaster management across three Caribbean small island states. The study quantifies OSM data gaps in Haiti, Dominica, and St. Lucia and demonstrates how coverage and quality directly affect evidence-based disaster risk assessment, humanitarian preparedness, and response planning in vulnerable island contexts.

image.alt: Graphical Abstract

author: Rann Goldblatt
Nicholas Jones
Jenny Mannix

use-cases.title: Mapping for Emergencies: WFP's custom mapping innovation informs humanitarian operations worldwide
use-cases.description: The World Food Programme uses OSM road network and settlement data through its Humanitarian Topographic Atlas to plan food aid delivery routes in crisis and post-disaster settings. OSM supports topographic mapping, logistics infrastructure visualization, and road network verification, while field teams in Syria and Nepal update OSM layers to guide truck drivers, identify access constraints, and coordinate humanitarian operations in remote and vulnerable areas.

image.alt: One of things humanitarians need during operations is a topographic map of the areas of operations. Photo: WFP/Rein Skullerud.

author: WFP Innovation Accelerator

use-cases.title: Organised Editing/Activities/Refugee-displaced Site Mapping for UNHCR
use-cases.description: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees uses OpenStreetMap to map refugee camps, displacement corridors, and border crossings in crisis zones. In Uganda, South Sudan, Bangladesh, and the Sahel, OSM data has been used to map camps like Bidi Bidi and Cox's Bazar, supporting service delivery, planning, and protection monitoring for displaced populations.
author: OpenStreetMap Wiki contributors

use-cases.title: OpenStreetMap Damage Assessment Review
use-cases.description: The American Red Cross uses OpenStreetMap for disaster damage assessment and field response coordination in crisis settings. During the response to Typhoon Haiyan in the Philippines in 2013, the American Red Cross directly coordinated volunteer mappers on OpenStreetMap, directing them to trace priority areas based on field intelligence.

image.alt: Typhoon Haiyan partially or totally destroyed roughly 1 million homes. Photo Credit: IFRC

author: Typhoon Haiyan (Yolanda) Interim Report

use-cases.title: CartONG provides GIS and mapping support to humanitarian organisations using OSM in crisis settings
use-cases.description: CartONG uses OpenStreetMap to support humanitarian operations such as camp management, needs assessments, and monitoring. It helps partners like UNHCR, Medecins Sans Frontieres, and Action Against Hunger apply OSM data, while building mapping capacity and tools for field teams.

image.alt: CartONG humanitarian GIS and OpenStreetMap support

author: CartONG

use-cases.title: NetHope supports NGO connectivity and digital tools including OSM-based mapping in disaster settings
use-cases.description: NetHope is a consortium of international NGOs enabling technology in humanitarian settings. It supports members like Save the Children, World Vision, and IRC with OSM-based mapping and connectivity tools. Through its Digital Humanitarian Network, NetHope integrates OSM into crisis response and builds NGO capacity for faster, higher-quality field operations.

image.alt: NetHope humanitarian technology and OpenStreetMap mapping support

author: NetHope

use-cases.title: Missing Maps: How geodata is helping humanitarian aid efforts
use-cases.description: Doctors Without Borders uses Missing Maps and OpenStreetMap to map underserved crisis areas before and during emergencies. Volunteers trace roads, buildings, and settlement features so MSF teams can plan water points, latrines, medical outreach, disease response, and logistics for displaced communities in hard-to-reach locations with faster situational awareness and coordination.

image.alt: Doctors Without Borders Missing Maps volunteers creating OpenStreetMap data for humanitarian response

author: Doctors Without Borders

use-cases.title: iMMAP embeds information management specialists in humanitarian operations, using OSM as a core data source
use-cases.description: iMMAP is an international non-profit providing information management support for humanitarian and development work. It embeds specialists in UN agencies, governments, and NGOs to produce maps and analyses. Using OpenStreetMap as a basemap, iMMAP builds dashboards tracking displacement, access constraints, and service availability in real time across global crisis contexts.

image.alt: iMMAP humanitarian information management using OpenStreetMap

author: Not specified

use-cases.title: Mapping as a response to the disaster in Esmeraldas, Ecuador
use-cases.description: In response to historic floods in Esmeraldas. Ecuador’s risk management agency and HOT hubs created targeted mapping projects for flooded zones. Volunteers combined river flood-susceptibility layers with populated areas to define priority zones and then traced roads and infrastructure in OSM

image.alt: Image view of flooded zones

author: Juan Melo Luna and Juan Arellano Valdivia

use-cases.title: Haiti Earthquake Crisis Mapping (2010)
use-cases.description: After Haiti’s 2010 earthquake, OpenStreetMap volunteers worldwide rapidly mapped roads and buildings from satellite imagery.  Over 450 OpenStreetMap volunteers across the world created the a detailed map of Haiti ever, which became the basemap for relief agencies such as UN, Red Cross, World Bank, US Marines who coordinated the responseh

image.alt: overlay of Haiti earthquake damage

author: OpenStreetMap contributors

use-cases.title: Nepal Earthquake Open Mapping (2015)
use-cases.description: The 2015 Nepal earthquake struck Nepal on 25th April. After the earthquake, More than 7,500 OpenStreetMap contributors from around the globe collaborated to map affected areas. This crowdsourced geodata (buildings, roads, villages) supported Nepal’s government, UN agencies, and NGOs in organizing rescue logistics and relief operations during the crisis.

image.alt: overlay of Nepal earthquake damage

author: OpenStreetMap contributors

use-cases.title: Hurricane Otis 2023 Response (HOT
use-cases.description: Hurricane Otis, a formidable Category 5 storm, struck Acapulco, Guerrero, Mexico, on October 25, 2023, after this disaster, the Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT) and community institutions initiated a project to address the critical need for accurate mapping and information.  Using satellite imagery, volunteers mapped 52,000+ buildings in Acapulco in days, tagging damages to guide rescue and relief.

image.alt: overlay of Hurricane Otis damage

author: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT)

use-cases.title: HOT West Africa Impact Report: Mapping and Open Data Critical to Disaster Response
use-cases.description: The Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT) released an impact report on September 18, 2024, highlighting two years of enhancing open data for disaster response in West and Northern Africa. Key successes include vital infrastructure mapping in Morocco, Senegal, Liberia, and Nigeria, showcasing the importance of accurate data and community collaboration.

image.alt: Image showing HOT's impact meeting in West Africa

author: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT)

use-cases.title: How Lyft discovered OpenStreetMap is the Freshest Map for Rideshare
use-cases.description: Lyft built its own in-house navigation platform entirely on OSM as its base map, using it to provide drivers and riders with precise ETAs and turn-by-turn directions. Their team has contributed over 330k changesets to OSM, including 45k missing roads in 2023 alone, to keep maps fresh for rideshare operations.

image.alt: An example of our sampling plan in Greater New York City. Mapillary dispatched cars to each of the S2 polygons and collected imagery along all driveable roads in each.

author: Clare Corthell

use-cases.title: OpenStreetMap as an efficient data source for mapping
use-cases.description: Komoot is used by 45 million outdoor enthusiasts to plan cycling, hiking, and mountain biking routes. It is built entirely on OSM data, using it for trail surfaces, access restrictions, and elevation-aware routing across most of Europe and beyond.

image.alt: Komoot outdoor cycling and hiking route planner using OpenStreetMap

author: Komoot For Business

use-cases.title: Organic Maps: Offline Hike, Bike, Trails and Navigation
use-cases.description: Organic Maps is a free, open-source offline navigation app powered entirely by OSM data. It provides turn-by-turn navigation for driving, cycling, and walking without any internet connection, data tracking, or ads, and reached 6 million installs by December 2025.

image.alt: Organic Maps offline navigation powered by OpenStreetMap

author: Organic Maps

use-cases.title: OsmAnd's Faster Offline Navigation with OSM Data
use-cases.description: OsmAnd is a feature-rich offline navigation app powered by OSM, supporting car, cycling, hiking, and boat navigation with contour lines, hillshading, and OSM editing built in. It allows users to contribute fixes back to OSM directly from the app.

image.alt: OsmAnd offline navigation app powered by OpenStreetMap

author: OsmAnd

use-cases.title: TTrufi Recognized by OSOR for Our Work in Urban Mobility
use-cases.description: Trufi builds open-source public transport apps for cities where informal routes are poorly documented. Its apps rely on OpenStreetMap for mapping data, helping communities collect minibus, bus, and shared mobility routes, convert them into usable transit information, and improve journey planning for daily commuters across underserved urban networks and regions.

image.alt: Trufi informal public transport routing app using OpenStreetMap data

author: Ted

use-cases.title: MGrabMaps for Ride-Hailing (Southeast Asia)
use-cases.description: Grab is a ride-share company, built its mapping platform using OpenStreetMap data. GrabMaps integrates hyper-local updates (new roads, one-ways, speed limits) into OSM’s base map, improving route planning and navigation for Grab’s drivers and delivery services across fast-changing Southeast Asian cities and to solve  the challenge of fast-changing road restrictions, new roads appearing overnight, and high traffic congestion.

image.alt: Map showing detections from images and probe data, and hyperlocal data

author: Adriana Lazar

use-cases.title: OpenTraffic: Real-Time Traffic Data with OSM
use-cases.description: The World Bank, Mapzen, and partners launched OpenTraffic, a project linking crowdsourced traffic speeds to OSM road segments. It processes anonymized GPS data (from taxis and apps) into free, real-time traffic speed datasets using OpenStreetMap’s road network

image.alt: OpenTraffic: Process map (Copyright: OpenTraffic.io)

author: Muthu Kumar

use-cases.title: OpenTripPlanner (Portland)
use-cases.description: OpenTripPlanner (Portland) is an open-source multimodal trip planning software system. It uses OpenStreetMap for its street and walking/cycling network combined with transit schedules (GTFS), allowing riders to plan door-to-door trips mixing buses, trains, biking, and walking

image.alt: OpenTripPlanner interface showing multimodal routing

author: OpenStreetMap wiki contributors

use-cases.title: CycleStreets: powering cyclists with OSM
use-cases.description: CycleStreets is a social enterprise, promoting cycling by the provision of UK-wide information on cycle-friendly routes, and various tools for cycle advocacy groups. It routing engine imports OSM road and cycleway data to plan safe bicycle routes.

image.alt: CycleStreets interface showing cycling routes

author: OpenStreetMap UK CIC

use-cases.title: Demystifying OpenStreetMap” (Streets.mn Podcast)
use-cases.description: A 2025 Streets.mn article/interview explains how OSM data powers many mobility apps. It notes that apps like Transit App use OSM for walking and biking directions. Community forks like OsmAnd and CoMaps provide offline navigation using OSM, offering alternative solutions.

image.alt: Streets.mn podcast

author: Ian R Buck

use-cases.title: CycleFix & MapComplete (OpenStreetMap US)
use-cases.description: OpenStreetMap US highlights community mapping for sustainable transportation, encouraging contributors to map bicycle and scooter infrastructure. Tools like MapComplete (an OSM-based mapping app) were developed for crowdsourcing bike parking and bike box lanes.

image.alt: CycleFix and MapComplete interface showing sustainable transportation mapping

author: OpenStreetMap US Staff

use-cases.title: Urban Form Analysis with OpenStreetMap Data - OSMnx
use-cases.description: OSMnx is a Python package developed by Geoff Boeing at USC that allows planners and researchers to download, model, and analyze street networks for any city in the world using OSM data. It is used for urban form analysis, walkability studies, accessibility modeling, and resilience assessments. It solves the challenge of making OSM street data easily accessible and analyzable for urban planning without writing extensive custom code.

image.alt: Each image was created with a single line of code. Adding in building footprints, we can see the texture, grain, and percolation of formal circulation networks in cities around the world, including informal settlements

use-cases.title: 15-Minute City Score Toolkit - Walkability Analytics
use-cases.description: This open-source QGIS and Python tool uses OSM pedestrian network and amenity data to measure how well a city conforms to the 15-minute city concept, where all essential services are reachable on foot within 15 minutes. It queries OSM for walkable streets and points of interest, calculates walking isochrones, and generates a scored walkability map for urban planners and policymakers.

image.alt: Interactive map of 15min City Score results generated with the 15min City Score Toolkit

author: Transform Transport

use-cases.title: Know Your City 2.0
use-cases.description: The Know Your City 2.0 project by HOT and Slum Dwellers International uses OSM, drone imagery, and the HOT Tasking Manager to map building footprints in slum and informal urban settlements across cities in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. The data supports community-led urban upgrading, land tenure, and infrastructure planning in areas that are otherwise invisible on official maps.

image.alt: Know Your City slum mapping project

author: OpenStreetMap Wiki contributors

use-cases.title: An OpenStreetMap derived building classification dataset for the United States
use-cases.description: Researchers developed a nationwide building classification dataset for the United States covering over 67 million buildings, using OSM building footprints and metadata as the primary data source. The dataset classifies buildings as residential or non-residential and supports urban planning applications including population estimation, energy use modeling, land use mapping, and digital twin construction.

image.alt: OpenStreetMap-derived building classification dataset for the United States

author: Henrique F. de Arruda
Sandro M. Reia
Shiyang Ruan
Kuldip S. Atwal
Hamdi Kavak
Taylor Anderson
Dieter Pfoser

use-cases.title: Worldwide Cityscapes Spatial Analysis Using OSM
use-cases.description: A project from the Institute for Advanced Architecture of Catalonia uses OSM data to conduct spatial analysis of urban form across cities worldwide. Using the Grasshopper Elk plugin, OSM road networks, building footprints, and land use data are extracted and analyzed for computational urban design and city comparison studies, addressing challenges of access to consistent global urban datasets.

image.alt: Worldwide cityscapes spatial analysis using OpenStreetMap data

author: Institute for Advanced Architecture of Catalonia

use-cases.title: A/B Street - Citizen-Friendly Street Redesign Simulation
use-cases.description: A/B Street turns OpenStreetMap road, lane, signal, parking, and transit-stop data into interactive traffic simulations. Citizens and planners can test street redesigns, bike lanes, bus priority, parking changes, and intersection edits, then compare how proposals affect drivers, cyclists, pedestrians, transit users, and neighborhood accessibility outcomes before implementation during public consultations.

image.alt: A/B Street urban traffic simulation built with OpenStreetMap data

author: Not specified

use-cases.title: 5 Open-Source Tools for Sustainable Multimodal Planning
use-cases.description: An American Planning Association article highlights five open-source tools for multimodal urban planning, including the Overture Maps Foundation platform, which integrates OSM data with additional sources from Esri and TomTom. The article addresses the challenge that incomplete data and lack of affordable planning tools have hampered communities from identifying and analyzing multimodal network improvements.

image.alt: Transit's "last mile" radius is widening, thanks to power-assisted scooters and bicycles. Photo by Lisa Nisenson.

author: Lian A. Plass
Isabel Youngs

use-cases.title: Streetlight mapping with OSM in Mexico
use-cases.description: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT) documents how Mexican communities use OSM to map streetlights and urban features. By integrating mapped roads, amenities, and green spaces into GIS, planners support local development, simulate urban growth, and identify priority areas for infrastructure and public-service improvements.

image.alt: Streetlight mapping in Mexico using OpenStreetMap

author: Celine Jacquin

use-cases.title: Birmingham City Council: leveraging traffic sensor data
use-cases.description: Birmingham City Council collaborated with the OpenStreetMap community to open up traffic sensor and road data. By publishing real-time sensor locations and mapping them in OSM, the council enabled open-source traffic modelling and predictive tools.

image.alt: Birmingham City Council OSM collaboration

author: OpenStreetMap UK CIC

use-cases.title: Map Kibera: Empowering Africa’s Biggest Slum with Collective Wisdom
use-cases.description: The Map Kibera project trained young residents of Kibera (Nairobi’s largest informal settlement) to create the first detailed map of their community on OpenStreetMap. They mapped roads, homes, clinics, water points, and even “black spots” where crimes occurred. This citizen map made Kibera visible to authorities and NGOs, one direct result was a new police post.

image.alt: Map Kibera community mapping project interface showing mapped roads and amenities in Kibera, Nairobi

author: Taka

use-cases.title: Long-Lasting Impact of Ramani Huria Project: A Look Back at How Community Map-Data Generated from 2015–2019 is Making a Difference for Disaster Responders in Dar es Salaam
use-cases.description: Ramani Huria (“Open Map” in Swahili) was a World Bank-funded community mapping project (2015–2020) in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. Local university students and residents mapped thousands of buildings, roads, and drains in flood-prone wards using OSM. These open maps have strengthened the city’s flood planning.

image.alt: Iddy Chazua conducting a river cross-section survey along the Msimbazi River using a locally made survey tool in RTK mode

author: OpenMap Development Tanzania

use-cases.title: Planning an Open Cities Mapping Project
use-cases.description: The World Bank launched the Open Cities Project in November 2012 which used OpenStreetMap to create to create open data ecosystems that will facilitate innovative, data-driven urban planning and disaster., Open Cities has brought together stakeholders from government, donor agencies, the private sector, universities, and civil society groups to create usable information through community mapping techniques.

image.alt: Planning an Open Cities Mapping Project report cover

author: World Bank group

use-cases.title: A Global Effort to Unleash Local Data: How Open Mapping is Shaping Grassroots Climate Resilience Worldwide
use-cases.description: The Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team uses OSM to support communities in mapping flood-prone areas, tracking deforestation, and building climate resilience. In Malawi in 2022, OSM communities mapped critical infrastructure damaged by Tropical Storm Ana and Cyclone Gombe to guide disaster recovery. In Ecuador in 2023, HOT coordinated with the national Risk Management Secretariat to map high-risk flooding areas ahead of the El Nino climate phenomenon.

image.alt: Grassroots climate resilience mapping using OpenStreetMap

author: Not specified

use-cases.title: CLIMADA + OSM-flex - Multi-Faceted Climate Risk Assessment
use-cases.description: Researchers at ETH Zurich developed OSM-flex, a Python tool that extracts features from OSM, including forests, railways, airports, UNESCO heritage sites, and healthcare facilities, and feeds them into the open-source CLIMADA climate risk platform. The tool was used to compute winter storm risk and event impacts from the severe 1999 storm Lothar across Switzerland, solving the challenge that most climate risk models only assess buildings and roads due to lack of open exposure data.

image.alt: Esri Basemap

author: ETH Zurich researchers

use-cases.title: Analysis of renewable energy infrastructure representations in OpenStreetMap
use-cases.description: Researchers evaluated the accuracy and completeness of wind and solar energy infrastructure data in OSM for Belgium and Ireland. By combining OSM power-tagged data with CORINE Land Cover inventory, they studied land use patterns around renewable energy installations to support environmental planning and the clean energy transition, addressing the challenge of scarce, reliable open-access data on renewable energy locations.
author: State of the Map 2024

use-cases.title: Built Environment Mapping for Urban Heat Island Analysis
use-cases.description: OpenStreetMap US launched a 2022 community mapping campaign to improve OSM data on impervious surfaces, including buildings, roads, and parking lots, for climate change analysis and urban heat island assessment in New Orleans, which ranked as the US city with the highest urban heat island index in 2021. The mapped data supports population risk assessments for sea level rise and extreme heat events.

image.alt: Built environment mapping for urban heat island analysis in New Orleans

author: OpenStreetMap US

use-cases.title: Open land cover from OpenStreetMap and remote sensing
use-cases.description: Researchers combined OpenStreetMap land-use data with remote sensing to build open land-cover products for environmental analysis. A Heidelberg case study filled OSM data gaps with satellite information, supporting land-use monitoring, ecological assessment, resource management, urban planning, and climate-change research workflows at local and regional scales across landscapes for policy decisions.

image.alt: Open land cover mapping using OpenStreetMap and remote sensing data

author: Not specified

use-cases.title: Using OpenStreetMap (OSM) to enhance the classification of local climate zones in the framework of WUDAPT
use-cases.description: Researchers developed a method to enhance Local Climate Zone classification, a system used in urban heat island studies and climate modeling, using OSM building and land use data. OSM data was integrated within the WUDAPT framework to improve characterization of urban thermal environments globally, supporting more accurate urban climate models such as WRF.
author: Cidália C. Fonte
Patrícia Lopes
Linda See
Benjamin Bechtel

use-cases.title: Mikel Maron: Crowdsourcing satellite imagery to document deforestation
use-cases.description: An investigative article (ExposingTheInvisible) interviews OSM founder Mikel Maron about the Logging Roads project. Volunteers mapped 30,532 logging roads in the Congo Basin using satellite imagery.

image.alt: logging roads in the Congo Basin

author: Mikel Maron

use-cases.title: Map4Mangrove: Collaborative Mapping for Mangrove Conservation and Climate Action - Indonesia
use-cases.description: The Map4Mangrove project. Led by HOT in partnership with local organizations, the project used OpenStreetMap (OSM) as the primary mapping platform to collect and update geospatial data on mangrove rehabilitation sites. Community members, students, and volunteers contributed by mapping coastlines, mangrove areas, waterways, and related infrastructure into OSM.

image.alt: The Blue Carbon Program's mangrove rehabilitation sites, each managed by local community groups

author: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT)

use-cases.title: Capturing opportunities for OpenStreetMap data to better support Anticipatory Action
use-cases.description: In the Philippines, volunteers mapped Butuan City in OSM to create a detailed basemap that mobile teams used to survey vulnerable businesses ahead of floods, enabling pre-positioning of support. OSM data was also used in Uganda to overlay flood extent with infrastructure and settlement maps for impact forecasting. HOT supports local data maintenance and connects response actors to OSM communities.

image.alt: Map of flood extent (for Ntoroko district in Uganda) identifying vulnerable areas, Source: 510

author: Sander Houstan, Katharina Lorenz, Carly Redhead, Sarah Lohr

use-cases.title: Mitigating heat stress by reducing solar exposure in pedestrian routing
use-cases.description: OpenStreetMap (OSM) was used as a core data source to extract the pedestrian network of Heidelberg, Germany. OSM road and sidewalk data were combined with high-resolution building and vegetation models to calculate solar exposure across the city’s walking paths. The study developed shade-optimized routing that directs pedestrians along routes with less sun exposure, reducing heat stress.

image.alt: Transformation of OSM road and sidewalk features from their original linear representations (a) to polygonal representations (b); (c) shows the resulting classification of the features based on the area fraction of the road surface that is exposed to the Sun

author: HeiGIT (Heidelberg Institute for Geoinformation Technology)

use-cases.title: Off the grid: utilizing OpenStreetMap for early warning and early action in conflict settings in Sudan
use-cases.description: OpenStreetMap (OSM) fill critical geospatial data gaps in conflict-affected Sudan. With limited official infrastructure maps, OSM provides base data for exposure and vulnerability analysis of populations affected by climate hazards. OSM’s crowd-sourced maps are positioned as key tools to identify high-risk areas, improve early action planning, and inform humanitarian decision-making.

image.alt: Daily edits of map nodes (created, modified, or deleted) in OpenStreetMap (OSM) in Sudan from 2012 to September 202

author: Cornelia Scholz
Catalina Jaime
Emmanuel Raju
Erin Coughlan De Perez
Maarten van Aalst

use-cases.title: Mapathons: OpenSource Mapping by GW Students
use-cases.description: Purdue University Libraries integrated OSM mapathons into undergraduate health sciences and data science learning communities as a tool for critical data literacy instruction. Since 2017, students have traced satellite imagery in OSM to create humanitarian maps, learning to evaluate data quality, geospatial ethics, and visual representation while contributing to meaningful real-world data creation within coursework.

image.alt: University OSM mapathons for data and visual literacy

author: Not specified

source.name: Journal of the Medical Library Association (PMC)

use-cases.title: Disastermappers Heidelberg - Training University Students in Humanitarian OSM Mapping
use-cases.description: The Disastermappers Heidelberg, hosted at Heidelberg University's GIScience Research Group, trains geography students in OSM-based humanitarian mapping for disaster preparedness and relief. The group organizes regular mapathons, contributes to Missing Maps, and conducts science-driven research on identifying poorly mapped vulnerable regions, embedding open mapping directly into university geoscience education.
author: Disastermappers Heidelberg

use-cases.title: The sweet spot for research and Missing Maps collaborations
use-cases.description: Missing Maps published a 2022 guide on how universities and research institutions can go beyond hosting mapathons to build deeper collaborations with humanitarian organizations using OSM. It highlights how academic research into OSM contributor behavior, data quality, and community engagement has directly improved humanitarian mapping workflows and outcomes for organizations like HOT and MSF.

image.alt: HOT Summit 2019

author: Benni Herfort
Sam Colchester

use-cases.title: Crowdsourcing missing maps at Geohub's Mapathon
use-cases.description: Central European University's GeoHub hosted a 2022 hybrid Missing Maps mapathon for International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction, gathering nearly 60 students and academics from CEU Vienna and AUCA Bishkek alongside online OSUN members. Participants received OSM training from a University of Maryland instructor and mapped vulnerable communities in the Dominican Republic, integrating disaster risk education with hands-on OSM contribution.

image.alt: CEU GeoHub Missing Maps mapathon with international students

author: Central European University

use-cases.title: YouthMappers Sri Lanka - Climate Activism and OSM Education
use-cases.description: YouthMappers chapters in Sri Lanka used OpenStreetMap workshops, curriculum integration, a mapping helpdesk, and a research unit to advance climate education. The project strengthened student capacity for open mapping, climate activism, disaster awareness, and locally relevant geospatial research at Eastern University and partner communities nationwide through collaboration and public service.

image.alt: YouthMappers Sri Lanka climate education and OpenStreetMap workshops

author: Ibra Lebbe Mohamed Zahir
Suthakaran Sundaralingam
Meerasa Lewai Fowzul Ameer
Sriram Sindhuja
Atham Lebbe Iyoob

use-cases.title: Mapathon @ TU Munich: Mapping At-Risk Regions with Awesome Mappers
use-cases.description: Founded in 2022, the YouthMappers chapter at Technical University of Munich organized a 2024 mapathon in response to the Turkey-Syria earthquake, partnering with the German Red Cross and Geoawesome. In a single session, 25 contributors produced 1,312 map edits and 1,198 building footprints for at-risk regions in Paraguay, demonstrating how university chapters build student OSM capacity for real-world disaster response.

image.alt: TU Munich YouthMappers mapathon for disaster response

author: Geoawesome

use-cases.title: Youth Mappers Unite for a Good Cause
use-cases.description: Youth Mappers network, a global student mapping movement using OpenStreetMap to support international development projects. University students from places like the U.S. and Bangladesh collaborate to create basemaps of under-mapped regions. These maps are used by USAID for planning food security, disease prevention, disaster response, and more, while students gain GIS and real-world problem-solving experience.

image.alt: George Washington University students map a rural area near Khulna, Bangladesh. The map will be used to support the US government's Feed the Future initiative there. Photo/Heather Blevins

author: Carla Wheeler

use-cases.title: Introducing Students to OpenStreetMap? Start Here
use-cases.description: TeachOSM is an educational initiative that promotes OpenStreetMap in classrooms worldwide. It provides tools, lesson plans, and training to students and teachers, enabling learners to contribute real OSM data. By integrating mapping projects into coursework, students gain geospatial skills while enhancing global open map data through community contributions
author: TeachOSM Team

use-cases.title: YouthMappers and World Bicycle Relief Project: University of Malawi (UNIMA) YouthMappers.
use-cases.description: University of Malawi YouthMappers partnered with World Bicycle Relief on the Bicycles for Growth project to improve rural mobility. Students mapped local bicycle infrastructure in Zomba district – logging bike shops, repair centers, and commonly used village pathways on OpenStreetMap.

image.alt: YouthMappers students mapping bicycle infrastructure in Malawi

author: Davie, Mphatso,Ishmael, Prince, Glory, Lemekezani, Constance & Abdur-rahman- University of Malawi (UNIMA

use-cases.title: OpenStreetMap Education in High School
use-cases.description: TeachOSM provides curriculum modules that enable teachers to incorporate OpenStreetMap into geography classes. AP Human Geography students participate in “mapathon” projects that tie into their coursework one class mapped farm-to-market roads in Guatemala on OSM to learn about rural economies.

image.alt: APGH Students using OpenStreetMap in the classroom

author: Celeste Reynolds

use-cases.title: Volunteer at Home: Make Digital Maps
use-cases.description: The Red Cross initiative invited volunteers to contribute to OpenStreetMap from home to help humanitarian response. Through the Missing Maps project, volunteers used OSM’s Tasking Manager to trace buildings and roads in under-mapped regions, improving disaster preparedness, vaccine campaigns, and emergency planning by providing open geographic data to responders.

image.alt: Red Cross steps

author: Kana Walsh,

use-cases.title: About OsmAnd routing
use-cases.description: OsmAnd is an offline maps and navigation app using OpenStreetMap data for global vector maps, search, points of interest, and voice-guided routing. It supports driving, cycling, walking, public transport, boating, skiing, and hiking profiles, helping users navigate cities and outdoor routes without mobile data across different regions reliably and planning.

image.alt: OsmAnd offline maps and turn-by-turn navigation powered by OpenStreetMap data

author: OsmAnd

use-cases.title: Organic Maps: Offline Hike, Bike, Trails and Navigation
use-cases.description: Organic Maps is a privacy-focused offline GPS app for travelers, hikers, cyclists, and drivers. Powered by OpenStreetMap data, it offers offline maps, search, bookmarks, and voice-guided walking, cycling, and car navigation, helping users plan routes without ads, tracking, or an active internet connection worldwide safely and privately during daily travel.

image.alt: Organic Maps offline GPS navigation using OpenStreetMap data

author: Organic Maps

use-cases.title: Komoot - Outdoor Route Planning for Cycling, Hiking, and Running
use-cases.description: Komoot uses OpenStreetMap data for outdoor route planning across hiking, running, road cycling, bike touring, and mountain biking. Its routing profiles, offline maps, voice navigation, tour recording, and trail-focused recommendations help users plan location-aware trips using OSM paths, roads, surfaces, and points of interest during everyday adventures confidently and recreation.
author: OpenStreetMap Wiki contributors

use-cases.title: GraphHopper Maps Route Planner
use-cases.description: GraphHopper Maps is a route-planning service for cycling, walking, driving, trucks, and outdoor trips. Built on OpenStreetMap, it provides alternative routes, surface and elevation details, multi-stop planning, GPX export, and mobile-friendly directions for travel, delivery, fieldwork, and logistics planning workflows across changing network conditions worldwide daily across real transport scenarios.

image.alt: GraphHopper Maps route planner built on OpenStreetMap

author: GraphHopper

use-cases.title: OpenRouteService - Directions, Geocoding, and Heavy Vehicle Routing
use-cases.description: OpenRouteService uses open geospatial data and OSM-derived street networks for directions, geocoding, isochrones, and route planning. It supports cars, bicycles, walking, hiking, wheelchairs, and heavy vehicles, helping teams plan deliveries, accessibility routes, emergency logistics, and location analysis around road restrictions in diverse operating contexts worldwide effectively and operational field decisions.

image.alt: OpenRouteService directions and location planning using OpenStreetMap street networks

author: OpenRouteService

use-cases.title: Valhalla - Open Source Turn-by-Turn Routing Engine
use-cases.description: Valhalla is an open-source routing engine that uses OpenStreetMap data to power turn-by-turn routing and navigation in web and mobile applications. It supports location inputs, costing models, multimodal routing, and navigation narratives, making OSM practical for custom route planners and location services at production scale worldwide for operational mobility products.
author: Not specified

use-cases.title: Mapbox Directions - Driving, Walking, and Cycling Route Planning
use-cases.description: Mapbox Directions API supports driving, walking, cycling, and traffic-aware route planning with turn-by-turn instructions. Its navigation tools help web and mobile products calculate routes, plan waypoints, match GPS traces to roads, and build location services using OpenStreetMap-derived map and road data for real-time user experiences reliably across modern navigation platforms.
author: Not specified

use-cases.title: Komoot for iPhone
use-cases.description: The Komoot iPhone app uses OpenStreetMap (OSM) data for map display and routing. It allows users to plan bike, hiking, and outdoor tours using OSM’s open geospatial database. Users can navigate routes, track activity, and, where supported, upload GPS tracks back into OSM, enhancing the underlying map data.

image.alt: Komoot iPhone app using OpenStreetMap data for navigation and route planning

author: Openstreetmap wiki contributors

use-cases.title: Apple Maps / Apple on OpenStreetMap Wiki
use-cases.description: Apple’s historical and regional use of OSM data. Apple has incorporated OpenStreetMap map tiles and geodata in its maps for some regions and applications since 2012. Apple’s mapping teams also contribute edits back to OSM, supporting global mapping coverage and improvements.

image.alt: Apple Maps using OpenStreetMap data for map display and navigation

author: OpenStreetMap Wiki contributors

use-cases.title: Launching the Facebook Mapping Platform with OpenStreetMap
use-cases.description: The Facebook global map project was built using OpenStreetMap (OSM) as its primary data source. Stamen partnered with Facebook engineers to design an open, scalable base map emphasizing community and local features. OSM data underpins global roads, parks, and landmarks, allowing Facebook to serve rich interactive maps for billions of users

image.alt: The new Facebook Map (map data © OpenStreetMap)

author: Jonah Adkins

use-cases.title: Pokémon Go Switches from Google Maps to OpenStreetMap
use-cases.description: In 2017 Niantic, the developer of Pokémon GO, replaced the game’s in-world map provider and base map data from Google Maps to OpenStreetMap (OSM). Before that change, Google Maps powered the visual map display while OSM data was occasionally used in background systems like spawn point logic.

image.alt: Pokémon GO game interface

author: Not specified

use-cases.title: Apple, Foursquare Ditch Google Maps for OpenStreetMap
use-cases.description: In 2012, Apple and Foursquare moved away from Google Maps toward OpenStreetMap to reduce costs, gain independence, and customize mapping features. The shift highlighted dissatisfaction with Google’s licensing limits.  OpenStreetMap provided Apple with local street and point-of-interest details, especially in countries or areas where proprietary data was lacking

image.alt: Apple and Foursquare using OpenStreetMap data for mapping and location services

author: John Rampton,

use-cases.title: Craigslist Baby-Steps Into Modernity
use-cases.description: In response to Google Maps API price hikes in 2012, several prominent web platforms migrated to OpenStreetMap. Craigslist, the popular classifieds site, began embedding OSM-based maps on housing listings (starting in SF and Portland) to show locations and save on fees.

image.alt: Craigslist using OpenStreetMap data for improved mapping and location services

author: Ryan Tate,
